data.description: Markwayne Mullin, a member of Congress, reported recent stock trades involving COF, CSX, ZTS, and PNR.

data.title: ServiceTitan's Q3 results exceeded expectations - earnings of 24 cents per share and sales of $249.2 million. FY2026 sales guidance increased to $951-$953 million.
data.description: ServiceTitan Inc (NASDAQ: TTAN) exceeded expectations with their third-quarter financial results, as they reported earnings of 24 cents per share compared to analyst consensus of 15 cents per share. Their quarterly sales of $249.200 million also surpassed the analyst estimate of $238.535 million. The company has raised their FY2026 sales guidance from $935.000 million-$940.000 million to $951.000 million-$953.000 million. The CEO expressed gratitude for the significant progress made in their first year of operation. The complete article can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Paramount's potential acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery sparks speculation as CNN stays independent, for now.
data.description: The potential acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery by Paramount prompted speculation among CNN reporters about a possible merger with CBS News. However, it has been decided that CNN will continue to operate as a distinct corporate entity.

data.title: Motor coach workers in Nevada join Teamsters Local 533. Drivers and dispatchers at Diversified Transportation now have union representation.
data.description: In a significant development in the mining transportation sector, motor coach drivers and dispatchers at Diversified Transportation, a subsidiary of Student Transportation of America (STA), have successfully obtained representation from Teamsters Local 533. This achievement marks a crucial win for workers in the industry, granting them a strong union voice. The decision was finalized in Reno, Nevada on December 5, 2025. The unionizing effort is expected to positively impact the working conditions and rights of approximately [insert number] individuals employed at Diversified Transportation.

data.title: ASK Academy Middle School Robotics Team placed 2nd in New Mexico state competition, advancing to national stage.
data.description: The Middle School Robotics Team from ASK Academy in Houston is preparing for the national competition after securing a second-place finish at the recent robotics event in Hobbs, New Mexico. Their impressive performance at the state level has earned them the opportunity to represent New Mexico at the national stage. The team's success highlights their dedication and skill in robotics, showcasing their potential on a larger platform. This achievement is a testament to their hard work and commitment to excellence in robotics competitions.

data.title: Shrub Oak International School in NY strengthens commitment to student safety with detailed security program for autistic children.
data.description: A school in Mohegan Lake, New York, revealed on December 5, 2025, new information about its safety measures for autistic individuals in its care. Shrub Oak International School introduced a robust safety and security program tailored to safeguard children, teenagers, and young adults with autism under its supervision. The program is multifaceted, aiming to ensure the well-being of its students.

data.title: CRI will showcase GaN-powered digital amplifier at CES 2026, redefining sound for mobility.
data.description: CRI Middleware Co., Ltd., a prominent developer of audio and video middleware, is set to introduce its innovative GaN-powered full digital amplifier at CES 2026 in Las Vegas. This low-heat and energy-efficient audio technology is specifically designed for next-generation vehicles. The product, known as CRI D-Amp, aims to revolutionize sound systems with its cutting-edge features. The debut is scheduled for December 5, 2025, and showcases a significant milestone in audio technology advancement.

data.title: Hormel Foods (NYSE: HRL) beat profit expectations with Q4 earnings of 32 cents per share, sales $3.185B fell short of $3.228B. Issued upbeat outlook for the year.
data.description: Hormel Foods Corporation (NYSE: HRL) exceeded Wall Street's profit expectations in the fourth quarter, though it fell short on revenue. The packaged foods company also provided a positive earnings and sales forecast for the upcoming year, indicating confidence in its turnaround despite facing ongoing cost pressures. In Q4, the company reported adjusted earnings per share of 32 cents, surpassing the consensus estimate of 30 cents. However, quarterly sales of $3.185 billion were below the expected $3.228 billion. The company's CEO noted solid top-line growth in fiscal 2025, driven by brand relevance and a strong value-added portfolio.

data.title: Compass Minerals likely to report Q4 loss of 23 cents per share, with revenue estimated at $223.72 million vs $208.8 million last year. Stock rose 1.7% to $20.12.
data.description: Compass Minerals International, Inc. (NYSE: CMP) is set to unveil its fourth-quarter earnings after the market closes on Monday, December 8. Analysts predict the company, based in Overland Park, Kansas, will post a loss of 23 cents per share for the quarter, an improvement from the 77-cent loss per share reported a year ago. The consensus among analysts is that Compass Minerals' quarterly revenue will be around $223.72 million, up from $208.8 million in the same period last year, as reported by Benzinga Pro. Following a disappointing third-quarter earnings report on August 11, which led to a reduction in its FY25 sales outlook, Compass Minerals International remains in focus. As of Thursday's market close, the company's shares had increased by 1.7% to reach $20.12. For further details, readers can refer to Benzinga.com.

data.title: Dollar General (NYSE: DG) beats Q3 expectations, reports $10.65B sales, $1.28 EPS, raises full-year guidance to $6.30-$6.50.
data.description: Dollar General Corporation (NYSE: DG) exceeded expectations with its third-quarter fiscal 2025 results, reporting net sales of $10.65 billion, slightly above the projected $10.64 billion. Earnings stood at $1.28 per share, surpassing the estimated 95 cents. The company has raised its full-year guidance for 2025 due to its strong performance in Q3 and improved outlook for the year, while also considering potential uncertainties in consumer behavior. Fiscal 2025 earnings guidance has been adjusted from $5.80-$6.30 to $6.30-$6.50, higher than the anticipated $6.17. Dollar General also raised its sales guidance for the year.

data.title: Oakworth Capital Bank maintains mid-90s Net Promoter Score, showcasing exceptional client satisfaction.
data.description: In Birmingham, Alabama on December 5, 2025, Oakworth Capital Bank (Oakworth) has once again proven its dedication to client trust and satisfaction. The bank has consistently achieved impressive Net Promoter Scores (NPS) in the mid-90s, showcasing a remarkable level of loyalty that is rarely seen in the financial services industry. This significant achievement underscores Oakworth's ongoing commitment to providing excellent service and meeting the needs of its clients.

data.title: Many US workers dread office holiday parties, with 27% preferring dental work. Disconnection worsens.
data.description: A recent national poll conducted in the United States uncovered that 27% of employees would choose to undergo dental work like a root canal procedure than participate in office celebrations. This finding sheds light on the increasing disengagement in workplaces across the country. The poll indicates a significant preference for dental work over office holiday parties, revealing a profound sense of disconnect among workers.

data.title: Encoded Therapeutics reports positive interim data for ETX101 gene therapy in Dravet syndrome at AES2025, featured in oral presentation and poster session.
data.description: In South San Francisco, California, a biotechnology company named Encoded Therapeutics Inc. shared optimistic interim findings from its POLARIS clinical trial on ETX101 for children suffering from SCN1A+ Dravet syndrome. These results were presented at the American Epilepsy Society (AES) Annual Meeting through an oral presentation today, with a follow-up poster session scheduled for tomorrow. The company is focused on developing genetic therapies for severe neurological conditions, with this particular program showing promise in addressing the disorder.

data.title: Cardano and Solana drop 7% and 5% respectively, XRP down 2%, approaching critical technical levels. Solana faces resistance around $141-$153.
data.description: XRP experienced a 2% decline on Friday as both Cardano (ADA) and Solana (SOL) saw continued drops in their prices. Solana saw a 5% decrease and is currently struggling to break above a downtrend line that has consistently blocked recovery attempts. The price of SOL sits below the trendline and the 20-day EMA around $140, indicating strong resistance in this area. Additionally, a cluster of Fibonacci resistance levels ranging from $141 to $153 has been preventing any significant upside movements. The Relative Strength Index (RSI) hovers around 41, indicating a slowdown in momentum without exhaustion signals, suggesting a gradual decline could persist. If selling pressure continues, the price might revisit the $122–$121 support range. For further details, the full story can be found on Benzinga.com.
